hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext:
I'm working on the following GitHub issue(s):

## Issue #78: Add optional Chat UI mode alongside native terminal view
Add a per-task toggle that lets users switch between the current native terminal view (xterm.js + node-pty) and a rendered chat UI for interacting with Claude Code — at any time, including mid-session.

The raw terminal works well but a chat UI would provide:
	∙	Clear visual separation between user prompts, assistant responses, and tool use
	∙	Rendered markdown (code blocks, lists, tables)
	∙	Collapsible tool use/result sections
	∙	A cleaner input experience (multiline compose box vs. terminal stdin)
Critically, if the chat UI ever breaks due to a Claude Code update or can’t handle a particular interaction, the user must be able to flip back to the terminal instantly — no lost context, no restart.

##% Vanilla approach
Two spawn modes, one session

``` 
Terminal mode (current):
  node-pty → PTY → claude (interactive)
                      ↓
                  xterm.js

Chat mode (new):
  child_process.spawn → piped stdin/stdout
    claude --output-format stream-json
                      ↓
              stream parser → React chat UI
```

Both modes use --session-id, so they share the same conversation history. Switching modes mid-session means:
	1.	Kill the current process (PTY or piped child)
	2.	Respawn Claude in the other mode with the same --session-id
	3.	Claude resumes the conversation from its persisted session
The toggle is a simple button on the task — “Switch to Chat” / “Switch to Terminal”. No data loss, no re-prompting.
Why this works
Claude Code sessions are file-backed (~/.claude/projects/<hash>/<session-id>.jsonl). The session ID is the continuity mechanism, not the process. Killing and respawning with the same session ID picks up where it left off. This means:
	∙	Chat → Terminal: If the stream parser chokes on an unexpected format, the user clicks one button and is back in the familiar terminal, same session.
	∙	Terminal → Chat: If the user wants a cleaner view of a long-running session, they can switch over and the chat UI reconstructs history from t...

Please help me implement a solution for this.
